---
title: "Content Contract"
page-layout: article
---

This site renders notebooks exactly as committed. The notebook files remain the source of truth.

Each notebook should start with Quarto YAML front matter in the first markdown cell:

```yaml
title: "Missing values, with TabPFN"
section: "Data preparation"
section-order: 10
order: 10
description: "How TabPFN behaves with common missing value patterns."
```

`section` is the sidebar group. `section-order` sorts groups using the familiarity order from the content plan. `order` sorts pages inside a group. Missing metadata is reported during render and the page is placed under `Needs metadata`.

## Counterfactuals

Counterfactual outputs must be cached in the notebook. The site only swaps already rendered results.

Use Quarto tabsets when the notebook prose owns the comparison:

```markdown
::: {.panel-tabset}
## easy-z

![easy-z result](figures/easy-z.png)

## hard-z

![hard-z result](figures/hard-z.png)
:::
```

Use the cached toggle when the result should feel like one control:

```html
<div class="cf-toggle" data-cf-knob="z-difficulty" data-cf-default="easy-z">
  <figure data-cf-setting="easy-z">
    <img src="figures/easy-z.png" alt="easy-z result">
    <figcaption>easy-z</figcaption>
  </figure>
  <figure data-cf-setting="hard-z">
    <img src="figures/hard-z.png" alt="hard-z result">
    <figcaption>hard-z</figcaption>
  </figure>
</div>
```

For notebook cell metadata, add these tags to every cached counterfactual output cell:

```text
counterfactual
cf-knob:z-difficulty
cf-setting:easy-z
```

Use the same `cf-knob` value for all settings in a knob family. Use one `cf-setting` value per cached result. If a notebook has counterfactual tags without both values, the render reports it.
